response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role requires deep hardware expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services and some C/C++ tooling work, which does not approach the principal-level hardware performance architecture scope. Years of experience satisfy the education path, but the domain mismatch is fundamental.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning workload, AI accelerator, or GPU architecture/performance background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or accelerator expertise
Low-latency systems programming and performance optimization exposure
